title: n64-quirk registry: known preserved retail bugs (e.g. chraidata.c:715 guard shuffle) not centrally documented

history.note: Triaged: the coverage gap is the absence of a central n64-quirk registry. docs/fidelity/QUIRKS.md now exists (charter 4.6) with the registry contract, retail anchor, player-visible effect, and opt-in mitigation flag per row.

Root cause: known preserved retail quirks were tracked only in scattered code comments and per-finding ledger notes with no single auditable index. The fix is the QUIRKS.md registry plus the ledger-first filing contract for new quirks.

QUIRKS.md populated: Q1 guard shuffle (chraidata.c 715) preserved as-is, and W1 stan.c uninitialized locals (FID-0081) waived as not reproducible on a host. Both currently-known n64-quirk findings are now registered.

QUIRKS.md committed with the preserved-quirk table, the waived not-reproducible sub-section, and the adjudicated non-quirks section; new quirks file as n64-quirk ledger findings and are added on anchor verification.

Coverage gap closed and verified: docs/fidelity/QUIRKS.md is the central registry, covers both known quirks (Q1 preserved, W1 waived FID-0081), and encodes the contract for future entries. Re-opens if a new n64-quirk finding lands without a registry row.
